question: [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransfer / Midnight.withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower using an ERC777 collateral token under the precondition that the receiver has a tokensReceived hook trigger the sequence withdrawCollateral -> isHealthy check passes -> safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets) -> receiver.tokensReceived() reenters Midnight.withdrawCollateral again, violating the invariant that a borrower's collateral balance must not go below the health threshold, causing scoped impact: double withdrawal of collateral via reentrancy leaves the borrower undercollateralized without triggering the health check on the second withdrawal? Proof idea: implement ERC777 receiver that reenters withdrawCollateral in tokensReceived; assert position.collateral[index] after both calls is less than health threshold allows.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransfer / Midnight.withdraw] Can an unprivileged lender using an ERC777 loan token under the precondition that the receiver has a tokensReceived hook trigger the sequence withdraw -> credit -= units -> withdrawable -= units -> safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, units) -> receiver.tokensReceived() reenters Midnight.withdraw again, violating the invariant that position.credit must not go below zero and withdrawable must not go below zero, causing scoped impact: double withdrawal of credit units via reentrancy drains more loan tokens than the lender is entitled to? Proof idea: implement ERC777 receiver that reenters withdraw in tokensReceived; assert withdrawable does not underflow and receiver_received <= original_credit.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / Midnight.repay] Can an unprivileged borrower using an ERC777 loan token under the precondition that the payer (msg.sender) has a tokensToSend hook trigger the sequence repay -> debt -= units -> withdrawable += units -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), units) -> payer.tokensToSend() reenters Midnight.repay again, violating the invariant that position.debt must not go below zero, causing scoped impact: double repayment via reentrancy reduces debt by 2*units while only transferring units tokens, creating free debt cancellation? Proof idea: implement ERC777 payer that reenters repay in tokensToSend; assert debt reduction equals actual tokens transferred.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransfer / Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator using an ERC777 collateral token under the precondition that the receiver has a tokensReceived hook trigger the sequence liquidate -> seize collateral accounting -> safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) -> receiver.tokensReceived() reenters Midnight.liquidate again on the same borrower, violating the invariant that a borrower's collateral must not be seized twice in the same transaction, causing scoped impact: double liquidation via reentrancy seizes more collateral than the borrower holds, draining other users' collateral from Midnight? Proof idea: implement ERC777 receiver that reenters liquidate in tokensReceived; assert total seized <= original collateral.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransfer (returndata check) / Midnight.withdraw] Can an unprivileged user under the precondition that the loan token returns a non-standard 1-byte returndata (e.g., 0x01) instead of a 32-byte bool on transfer trigger the sequence withdraw -> safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, units) -> (bool success, bytes memory returndata) = token.call(...) -> success=true -> returndata.length == 1 -> abi.decode(returndata, (bool)) reverts with ABI decoding error, violating the invariant that a successful ERC20 transfer with truthy return must not cause safeTransfer to revert, causing scoped impact: all withdrawals, collateral withdrawals, fee claims, and liquidations are permanently DoS-ed for tokens with 1-byte return values? Proof idea: deploy token returning bytes1(0x01); call withdraw; assert revert is ABI decode error not TransferReturnedFalse.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom (returndata check) / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the loan token returns a non-standard 1-byte returndata on transferFrom trigger the sequence take -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) -> success=true -> returndata.length == 1 -> abi.decode(returndata, (bool)) reverts, violating the invariant that a successful transferFrom with truthy return must not cause safeTransferFrom to revert, causing scoped impact: all take, repay, supplyCollateral, and liquidate operations are permanently DoS-ed for tokens with 1-byte return values? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransfer (code check) / Midnight] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that a loan token contract is selfdestructed (via SELFDESTRUCT opcode on a pre-Cancun chain or via a proxy upgrade that removes code) between the require(token.code.length > 0) check and the token.call(...) in safeTransfer trigger the sequence safeTransfer -> code.length > 0 passes -> token selfdestructs in same block -> token.call returns success=true with empty returndata (call to EOA succeeds) -> returndata.length == 0 passes, violating the invariant that a successful safeTransfer must result in actual token movement, causing scoped impact: Midnight records a withdrawal/fee claim/liquidation as successful but no tokens are transferred, draining accounting without moving funds? Proof idea: simulate selfdestruct between code check and call; assert token balance unchanged after safeTransfer returns success.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that referralFeePct is set such that referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) > maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets trigger the sequence take loop -> referralFeeAssets computation -> safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets), violating the invariant that maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets must be non-negative, causing scoped impact: arithmetic underflow reverts the entire bundle even though all takes succeeded, DoS-ing the user? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct near WAD-1 and filledBuyerAssets near maxBuyerAssets; assert underflow condition.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that minSellerAssets is set to filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets and the loan token is fee-on-transfer trigger the sequence take loop -> filledSellerAssets computed -> require(filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets >= minSellerAssets) passes -> safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets) fails because bundler holds filledSellerAssets*(1-f), violating the invariant that passing the minSellerAssets check guarantees the receiver will receive at least minSellerAssets, causing scoped impact: user is misled into believing they will receive minSellerAssets but the actual transfer reverts? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the loan token is fee-on-transfer and targetSellerAssets is set such that targetFilledSellerAssets = targetSellerAssets + referralFeeAssets trigger the sequence take loop fills to filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets -> require(filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets) passes -> safeTransfer(loanToken, referralFeeRecipient, referralFeeAssets) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, targetSellerAssets), violating the invariant that filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets guarantees bundler holds enough to pay both transfers, causing scoped impact: bundler holds filledSellerAssets*(1-f) < filledSellerAssets, causing the second safeTransfer to revert or drain residual balance? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.pullToken] Can an unprivileged user under the precondition that the token is a rebasing token that increases balances between pullToken and the subsequent Midnight interaction trigger the sequence pullToken(token, user, amount) -> bundler receives amount -> token rebases upward -> forceApproveMax -> Midnight.safeTransferFrom(token, bundler, address(this), amount) -> bundler sends amount but holds amount + rebase_delta, violating the invariant that bundler must not accumulate residual balances from rebasing tokens, causing scoped impact: residual rebase_delta remains in bundler permanently, becoming extractable by the next caller who triggers a refund or transfer? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / Midnight.take (two-transfer pattern)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the loan token is fee-on-transfer with fee f trigger the sequence take -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) -> Midnight receives (buyerAssets-sellerAssets)*(1-f) -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets) -> receiver receives sellerAssets*(1-f), violating the invariant that the seller (maker) must receive exactly sellerAssets loan tokens, causing scoped impact: maker receives sellerAssets*(1-f) < sellerAssets, losing f*sellerAssets per take, constituting direct user loss for makers using fee-on-transfer loan tokens? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer loan token; call take; assert maker_received < sellerAssets.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / Midnight.take (payer = buyerCallback)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that buyerCallback is set and the loan token is fee-on-transfer trigger the sequence take -> payer = buyerCallback -> onBuy callback executes -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, buyerCallback, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) -> Midnight receives less than buyerAssets - sellerAssets -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, buyerCallback, receiver, sellerAssets) -> receiver receives less than sellerAssets, violating the invariant that the settlement fee accounting claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] += buyerAssets - sellerAssets must be backed by actual received tokens, causing scoped impact: claimableSettlementFee grows faster than actual token balance, making fee claims revert? Proof idea: use fee-on-transfer token with buyerCallback; assert claimableSettlementFee > actual balance after N takes.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransfer / Midnight.withdraw (multi-user insolvency)] Can an unprivileged lender under the precondition that multiple lenders have credit in a market using a fee-on-transfer loan token trigger the sequence lender A calls withdraw(units_A) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, A, units_A) -> Midnight sends units_A but receives units_A*(1-f) less from repayments -> lender B calls withdraw(units_B) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, B, units_B) fails because Midnight's balance is insufficient, violating the invariant that all lenders with valid credit must be able to withdraw, causing scoped impact: last lenders to withdraw are unable to redeem their credit, constituting fund freeze? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget (collateral supply + sell atomicity)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the collateral token is fee-on-transfer trigger the sequence pullToken(collateralToken, msg.sender, assets) -> bundler receives assets*(1-f) -> forceApproveMax(collateralToken, MIDNIGHT) -> supplyCollateral(market, index, assets, taker) -> Midnight.safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, bundler, address(this), assets) fails because bundler holds assets*(1-f) < assets, violating the invariant that a user who approved the bundler to pull assets collateral must be able to supply assets to Midnight, causing scoped impact: the entire bundle reverts, DoS-ing the user's intended borrow flow even though they paid the full collateral amount? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransfer / MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral (collateral withdrawal after fee-on-transfer buy)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the loan token is fee-on-transfer and collateralWithdrawals is non-empty trigger the sequence take loop succeeds (bundler has enough for takes) -> withdrawCollateral(market, index, assets, taker, collateralReceiver) -> safeTransfer(collateralToken, collateralReceiver, assets) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets) fails because bundler's loan token balance is insufficient, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal success does not guarantee loan token refund success, causing scoped impact: collateral is withdrawn from Midnight but the loan token refund reverts, reverting the entire tx and leaving the user confused about state? Proof idea: use fee-on-transfer loan token; include collateral withdrawal; assert revert after collateral withdrawal.
